headline: Microsoft-OpenAI Partnership Restructured: End of Azure Exclusivity, AGI Clause Removed
raw_snippet: OpenAI and Microsoft announced an amended partnership agreement on April 27 that ends Azure exclusivity (OpenAI can now serve products across any cloud provider), removes the AGI clause entirely, restructures revenue sharing (Microsoft no longer pays revenue share to OpenAI; OpenAI's payments to Microsoft continue through 2030 with a cap), and extends Microsoft's license to OpenAI IP through 2032. Microsoft remains primary cloud partner with products shipping first on Azure.
curator_notes: Biggest structural change to OpenAI's business model since inception. Ends Azure lock-in, removes AGI kill switch, opens multi-cloud revenue. Top-tier IB relevance.

headline: OpenAI Models, Codex, and Managed Agents Launch on Amazon Bedrock
raw_snippet: AWS and OpenAI expanded their partnership with three new offerings on Amazon Bedrock in limited preview: GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.4 models, Codex coding agent, and Amazon Bedrock Managed Agents powered by OpenAI. Usage can be applied toward existing AWS cloud commitments and inherits enterprise controls including IAM, PrivateLink, guardrails, and CloudTrail logging.
curator_notes: Immediate execution on exclusivity end — AWS launched within 24 hours. Includes GovCloud for government customers. Shows multi-cloud strategy is live, not theoretical.

headline: OpenAI Signs Classified AI Deal with Pentagon for 'Any Lawful Operational Use'
raw_snippet: The U.S. Department of Defense signed deals with seven AI companies including OpenAI to use their technology on classified networks. OpenAI agreed to let the Pentagon use its AI software for any 'lawful operational use.' The announcement comes after Anthropic declined to grant similar broad license and was banned from Defense Department work.
curator_notes: Major government revenue stream. Competitive advantage over Anthropic which refused. 'Any lawful use' is a broad license — signals willingness to work with defense.

headline: WSJ: OpenAI Misses Revenue and User Growth Targets Ahead of Planned IPO
raw_snippet: The Wall Street Journal reported that OpenAI missed internal targets for both user growth and revenue. The company fell short of its goal of 1 billion weekly active ChatGPT users (reaching 900M by Feb 2026) and missed multiple monthly revenue targets in early 2026, losing ground to Anthropic (which crossed $30B annualized revenue) and Google Gemini. CFO Sarah Friar warned that if revenue growth doesn't accelerate, the company could face difficulty funding future compute agreements.
curator_notes: Critical IB signal. Revenue miss + Anthropic overtaking + $600B commitment overhang. Triggered 7-10% selloffs in Oracle, CoreWeave, SoftBank.

headline: OpenAI Finalizes $10B Joint Venture 'The Deployment Company' with PE Consortium
raw_snippet: OpenAI finalized a $10 billion joint venture called 'The Deployment Company' with 19 PE investors including TPG, Brookfield, Advent, Bain Capital, SoftBank, and Dragoneer. OpenAI commits up to $1.5B ($500M equity at close + $1B option). The venture embeds OpenAI engineers directly inside client organizations across healthcare, logistics, manufacturing, and financial services. OpenAI retains control through super-voting shares with a 17.5% guaranteed annual return for investors over five years.
curator_notes: Massive PE deal. 17.5% guaranteed return is unusual. Palantir forward-deployed model at AI scale. Anthropic launched rival JV same day with Blackstone/Goldman.

headline: Brockman Testifies OpenAI Will Spend $50 Billion on Computing in 2026
raw_snippet: OpenAI President Greg Brockman testified on the witness stand that the company expects to spend $50 billion on computing power in 2026 to support its AI software, up from roughly $30 million in 2017. The disclosure came during the Musk v. Altman trial and reflects the extraordinary capital intensity of frontier AI development.
curator_notes: First on-the-record confirmation of 2026 compute budget. $50B/year capex unprecedented for pre-IPO company. Critical for infrastructure investors.

headline: OpenAI Plans Massive Expansion of Cheaper ChatGPT Go Tier, Expects 112M Subscribers
raw_snippet: OpenAI forecasts that subscribers to ChatGPT Go ($8/month in US, ~$5/month elsewhere) will surge 36x to 112 million this year, while ChatGPT Plus subscribers are projected to fall 80% to about 9 million. The company expects to offset revenue decline with advertising shown to ChatGPT Go users, projecting ad revenue per user to rise to $59 by 2030.
curator_notes: Major strategic pivot from premium to volume + ads. 80% Plus subscriber decline alarming but 112M Go subs at $8/mo = ~$10.8B ARR. Revenue model transformation.

headline: OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar Pushes to Delay IPO to 2027; Altman Wants 2026 Listing
raw_snippet: OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar is advocating to delay the company's IPO to 2027, citing $600 billion in accumulated infrastructure spending commitments and missed growth targets. CEO Sam Altman is pushing for a Q4 2026 listing targeting a $1 trillion valuation. A 2027 listing would give time to close the gap between infrastructure commitments and revenue and resolve the Musk litigation.
curator_notes: Internal C-suite tension on IPO timing. $600B commitment overhang + missed targets = CFO caution. Direct signal for IB teams working potential mandate.

headline: S&P 500 Considers Rule Change That Could Fast-Track OpenAI IPO Index Entry
raw_snippet: S&P Dow Jones Indices is consulting on whether to let large stocks into indexes after just 6 months post-IPO (down from 12 months) and exempt mega-cap companies from profitability requirements. The rule change could boost demand for eventual IPO shares of OpenAI, Anthropic and SpaceX. Consultation period open until May 28.
curator_notes: Structural market change benefiting OpenAI IPO. Faster index inclusion = more passive fund buying. Important for IPO underwriting teams.

headline: Microsoft AI Revenue Tops $9.25B Quarterly, 123% YoY Growth
raw_snippet: Microsoft disclosed for the first time that its total AI revenue exceeded $9.25 billion in quarterly revenue, 123% higher year-over-year. This includes sales of specialized AI servers on Azure to OpenAI and other AI firms. Microsoft did not specify what percent of its $627B revenue backlog came from OpenAI, after saying last quarter that the startup accounted for 45%.
curator_notes: Quantifies OpenAI's contribution to Microsoft ecosystem. 45% of $627B backlog from OpenAI last quarter = enormous mutual dependence.

headline: Musk v. Altman Trial Opens in Oakland: Musk Testifies Over Three Days
raw_snippet: The Musk v. Altman trial opened April 28 in federal court in Oakland before Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers with a nine-person jury. Musk alleges OpenAI, Altman and Brockman breached promises to keep the AI lab a nonprofit, seeking $150 billion in damages and the ouster of leadership. Musk testified over three days that he donated $38 million under the premise the organization would prioritize safe AI development.
curator_notes: Dominant story of the week. $150B damages claim + leadership ouster = existential legal risk. Advisory jury; ruling by Judge Gonzalez Rogers expected mid-May.

headline: Musk Admits Under Oath That xAI Distilled OpenAI Models to Train Grok
raw_snippet: During cross-examination on April 30, Elon Musk acknowledged it is 'partly' true that xAI used OpenAI models to train its own Grok AI through a process known as distillation. Musk downplayed the admission, calling it 'standard practice,' but the revelation undercuts his claim of being harmed by OpenAI's for-profit pivot while his own company benefited from their technology.
curator_notes: Material trial revelation. Undermines Musk's standing — benefited from the very IP he claims was misappropriated. Could influence liability ruling.

headline: Brockman Discloses Nearly $30 Billion OpenAI Stake; Testifies Musk Used Staff for Tesla
raw_snippet: OpenAI co-founder and President Greg Brockman disclosed during trial testimony that his stake in the firm is worth nearly $30 billion. He also testified that Musk enlisted several OpenAI employees to do months of unpaid work for Tesla Autopilot in 2017 without organizational awareness, rebutting Musk's framing of selfless commitment to OpenAI's mission.
curator_notes: Two key revelations: $30B stake implies valuation math, Musk using OpenAI staff for Tesla damages his credibility. Both material for trial outcome.

headline: Musk Sought Settlement with OpenAI Two Days Before Trial; Sent Threatening Texts
raw_snippet: A court filing revealed that Elon Musk sent a message to OpenAI President Greg Brockman on April 25 'to gauge interest in settlement.' When Brockman suggested both sides drop their claims, Musk responded: 'By the end of this week, you and Sam will be the most hated men in America.' OpenAI claims these texts show Musk's motivations are personal, not about protecting the nonprofit mission.
curator_notes: Shows Musk uncertainty pre-trial. Threatening tone could influence jury perception. Settlement attempt suggests weaker legal position.

headline: GPT-5.5 Instant Becomes Default ChatGPT Model With Major Accuracy Gains
raw_snippet: OpenAI replaced its ChatGPT default model with GPT-5.5 Instant, succeeding GPT-5.3 Instant. Internal testing shows 52.5% fewer hallucinated claims on high-stakes medical, legal, and financial prompts, and 37.3% fewer inaccurate claims on previously flagged conversations. Responses use ~30% fewer words and cut back on emoji usage. Available to all ChatGPT users and via the API.
curator_notes: Significant model upgrade — 52.5% hallucination reduction on high-stakes prompts material for enterprise adoption. Competitive positioning vs Anthropic/Google.

headline: OpenAI Publishes 'Where the Goblins Came From' — RLHF Bug Post-Mortem
raw_snippet: OpenAI published a technical blog explaining how starting with GPT-5.1, models developed a habit of increasingly mentioning goblins and gremlins in metaphors (usage up 175%). Root cause: the 'Nerdy personality' customization feature gave disproportionate RLHF rewards for creature metaphors, which then spread beyond the Nerdy condition due to reinforcement learning not staying scoped.
curator_notes: Rare technical transparency on RLHF failure mode. Shows maturity of safety practices. Less direct IB relevance but signals operational rigor.

headline: OpenAI Introduces Advanced Account Security with Passkeys and YubiKey Partnership
raw_snippet: OpenAI launched Advanced Account Security, an opt-in feature for ChatGPT accounts that requires passkeys or security keys, disables password/SMS/email sign-in, adds recovery keys, shortens sessions, and automatically excludes conversations from training data. Recommended for journalists, researchers, and political dissidents. OpenAI partnered with Yubico for preferred pricing on customized YubiKey bundles.
curator_notes: Enterprise security feature for high-risk users. Yubico partnership signals enterprise hardening. Mandatory for gov/defense users from June 1.

headline: OpenAI Sales Leader James Dyett Departs for Operator in Residence Role at Thrive Capital
raw_snippet: OpenAI sales leader James Dyett announced he is leaving to become Operator in Residence at Thrive Capital, one of OpenAI's longterm backers whose founder Joshua Kushner has a close relationship with CEO Sam Altman. Dyett joined OpenAI in 2023 and led enterprise sales and API sales during a period of explosive growth following ChatGPT's launch.
curator_notes: Continues senior executive exodus. Sales leader departure notable given revenue miss concerns. Move to key investor's firm suggests ongoing relationship.

headline: AI-Adjacent Stocks Plunge on OpenAI Revenue Miss: Oracle -7.7%, CoreWeave -7.4%, SoftBank -10%
raw_snippet: The WSJ report on OpenAI's missed targets triggered a significant selloff in AI infrastructure stocks. Oracle dropped 7.7%, CoreWeave fell 7.4%, and SoftBank sank nearly 10% in Tokyo trading.
cut_reason: Clustered with revenue miss item — market reaction captured in corroborating_urls

headline: OpenAI Responds to Revenue Miss: 'Firing on All Cylinders'
raw_snippet: OpenAI pushed back on the WSJ revenue miss report, calling it 'ridiculous'.
cut_reason: Clustered with revenue miss item — PR response, not independent news

raw_snippet: The Washington Post's trial analysis notes week one was dominated by personality rather than AI governance.

raw_snippet: Fortune's legal analysis argues the trial has failed to advance AI control questions.

headline: ChatGPT Adds Model Selection in Composer
raw_snippet: ChatGPT added model selection in the composer, allowing users to find and switch models from the same place where they write their prompt.
cut_reason: Trivially minor UX change — model picker relocation not MD-worthy

headline: OpenAI Technical Blog on Delivering Low-Latency Voice AI at Scale
raw_snippet: OpenAI engineers published a deep technical blog on how they rebuilt the WebRTC stack for voice AI.
cut_reason: Pure engineering blog — WebRTC infrastructure details not material for IB audience

headline: How ChatGPT Serves Ads: Technical Details Revealed
raw_snippet: OpenAI's ad platform uses Fernet-encrypted click tokens for full attribution.
cut_reason: Obscure source, purely technical teardown of ad system — not news for IB audience

headline: Microsoft Shifts to Usage-Based Pricing for AI Products
raw_snippet: Microsoft CEO Nadella said any per-user business will become 'per-user and usage' as AI drags down cloud margins.
cut_reason: Primarily about Microsoft pricing strategy — OpenAI mentioned only as inference cost driver

headline: Anthropic Launches Rival PE Joint Venture With Blackstone, Goldman Sachs
raw_snippet: Anthropic announced its own PE-backed joint venture with Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, and Goldman Sachs.
cut_reason: Story about Anthropic, not OpenAI — competitive context captured in Deployment Company item
